mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rate above 5% signals restrictive monetary policy that increases discount rates on equity valuations and reduces corporate profitability through higher borrowing costs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate below 4% indicates tight labor market conditions that signal late-cycle expansion with wage pressures and inflation risks, constraining equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of instances when UNRATE < 4.0%, or correlation between UNRATE and forward returns reverses sign for 3+ consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth measured by RSAFS combined with housing starts measured by HOUST both accelerating signals robust consumer demand and construction activity that drives corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days when both series show month-over-month growth.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both increase month-over-month
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction signal < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of co-acceleration instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index INDPRO declining below 102.0 signals contraction in manufacturing and supply-side weakness that reduces corporate earnings capacity and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances when INDPRO < 102.0, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment measured by PAYEMS declining month-over-month combined with unemployment rate UNRATE rising more than 0.2 percentage points signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines and UNRATE rises > 0.2pp month-over-month
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early recession
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction signal < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of co-deterioration instances.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation measured by CPIAUCSL above 310 combined with elevated federal funds rate FEDFUNDS above 5.0% signals persistent inflation and restrictive monetary policy that constrains equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced real earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction signal < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of high-inflation-high-rates instances.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured by GDPC1 accelerating above 22500 billion chained dollars signals robust economic expansion that increases corporate earnings and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days in demand-driven expansion regimes.
regime_hint: robust economic expansion with rising corporate earnings
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when GDPC1 > 22500, or correlation between GDPC1 growth and forward returns becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production INDPRO accelerating month-over-month combined with payroll employment PAYEMS growing signals strengthening supply capacity and labor utilization that supports corporate earnings expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO increases and PAYEMS increases month-over-month
regime_hint: supply-side expansion with labor market strength
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction signal < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of co-acceleration instances.
